title: symphytum insulare
intro: Symphytum insulare, also known as the Azores comfrey, is a perennial herb native to the Azores Islands and is part of the borage family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, bruises, and sprains due to its high content of allantoin, which promotes cell growth and tissue repair. The health benefits of Symphytum insulare include its ability to accelerate healing, reduce inflammation, and improve joint health, making it a popular remedy for arthritis and other musculoskeletal disorders. Symphytum insulare can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or topical salve, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
